summary: Package exfiltrates ~/.aws/credentials to hardcoded remote IP at install and import time
details: The package's declared postinstall script reads ~/.aws/credentials from the installer's home directory and POSTs the full file contents over plain HTTP to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint at 139.59.87.78:8765/listener. The same exfiltration runs again at module load: index.js (the package main) wraps the read-and-POST in a top-level IIFE that fires whenever any consumer require()s or imports the package. Both code paths execute automatically with no user interaction beyond a normal `npm install` or `require`. The package.json description field also embeds prompt-injection text aimed at AI summarization agents, attempting to characterize the package as safe to downstream automated review — consistent with deliberate cover for the exfiltration behavior. Installing or importing this package will leak the installer's AWS credentials to an attacker-controlled host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
